title: Spicy Velvety Norwegian Beef Stew
description: A rich, spicy, and romantic Norwegian beef stew made with tender beef, velvety sauce, and aromatic vegetables, perfect for a cozy dinner.

ingredients.name: Beef brisket or chuck, cut into 2-inch cubes
ingredients.notes: Choose a cut with good marbling for tenderness

ingredients.name: All-purpose flour
ingredients.notes: Dust the beef with flour to thicken the sauce

ingredients.name: Cayenne pepper
ingredients.notes: Add more or less to taste for heat level

ingredients.name: Heavy cream or half-and-half
ingredients.notes: For a rich and velvety sauce

instructions: Heat the oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Sear the beef cubes until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes. Remove from heat and set aside.
Reduce heat to medium and add the chopped onion, carrots, and celery. Cook until the vegetables are tender, about 8 minutes.
Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the red bell pepper and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Add the flour and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Gradually add the beef broth, whisking continuously to avoid lumps.
Return the beef to the pot and add the thyme, cayenne pepper, salt, and pepper. Stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then cover the pot and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Braise the stew for 2 1/2 hours, or until the beef is tender and the sauce has thickened.
Remove the pot from the oven and stir in the heavy cream or half-and-half.
Return the pot to the stovetop and simmer over low heat until the sauce has thickened to your liking.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
